channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: UK Visa Portal spilled thousands of applicants’ passports and selfies online — and hasn’t fixed the leak
item.description: The third-party website exposed applicants' sensitive documents as part of the U.K. visa application process. Instead of fixing the issue, the company sent attorneys.

item.title: Corsair's Pro lineup is the company’s answer to the growing demand for AI workstations and servers
item.description: The Corsair Pro lineup features various configurations, including Nvidia's Grace Blackwell Ultra (GB300) GPUs.

item.title: Pope Leo Schooled the Tech Bros on Tolkien
item.description: The Holy Father referenced The Lord of the Rings in his encyclical about AI—an expert (if unintentional) troll of tech billionaires who keep misinterpreting the series.

item.title: NYT Strands hints and answers for Wednesday, May 27 (game #815)
item.description: Looking for NYT Strands answers and hints? Here's all you need to know to solve today's game, including the spangram.

item.title: Quordle hints and answers for Wednesday, May 27 (game #1584)
item.description: Looking for Quordle clues? We can help. Plus get the answers to Quordle today and past solutions.

item.title: NYT Connections hints and answers for Wednesday, May 27 (game #1081)
item.description: Looking for NYT Connections answers and hints? Here's all you need to know to solve today's game, plus my commentary on the puzzles.

item.title: What we’re looking for in Startup Battlefield 2026, and how to apply in time for the May 27 deadline
item.description: Startup Battlefield applications are due tomorrow, so now's the time to put the finishing touches on your submission!

item.title: Trump Admin permits Volvo to keep selling connected cars in the U.S.
item.description: Volvo, which is majority owned by China's Geely Holdings, said it can now move forward with its expansion plans for its U.S. factory.

item.title: Watching the World Cup is the one time I’d use motion smoothing on a TV — for streaming movies, I avoid this setting like the plague
item.description: Motion smoothing should normally be avoided when watching movies or shows, yet enabling it for sports can be beneficial.

item.title: Sony abruptly shuts down online multiplayer game Destruction AllStars
item.description: Hope you weren't planning to queue up for the PS5 launch window title.

item.title: Trump administration wants nuclear startups to use plutonium for their reactors
item.description: The U.S. government is sitting on dozens of tons of weapons-grade plutonium. It's hoping startups can find a use for it.

item.title: DuckDuckGo installs are up 30% as users reject being ‘force-fed’ Google’s AI Search
item.description: Google overhauled Search at I/O 2026, replacing blue links with AI agents. The backlash has been swift. DuckDuckGo app installs spiked 30% as users seek a way out.

item.title: DeepSWE blows up the AI coding leaderboard, crowns GPT-5.5, and finds Claude Opus exploiting a benchmark loophole
item.description: For months, the leading AI coding benchmarks have told enterprise buyers a comforting but misleading story: the top models are all roughly the same. OpenAI's GPT-5 family, Anthropic's Claude Opus, and Google's Gemini Pro have clustered within a narrow band on Scale AI's SWE-Bench Pro leaderboard, making it nearly impossible for engineering leaders to determine which agent will actually perform best inside their codebases.On Monday, a startup called Datacurve released a benchmark it says shatters that illusion. DeepSWE, a 113-task evaluation spanning 91 open-source repositories and five program...

item.title: NASA’s permanent Moon base plans start with three missions this year
item.description: On Tuesday, NASA announced several upcoming lunar missions to the Moon's South Pole region. These missions will pave the way for the crewed Artemis landing slated for 2028, starting with three Moon Base missions NASA says are "the first of more than a dozen missions that will be announced this year." In addition to the […]
